zh.'s-Hertogenbosch, North Brabant, Netherl: 荷蘭努-布拉班特也-黑達歐根包斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth於1370年1月出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特利斯時，她的父親Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel37歲，母親Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts10歲。她和Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen至少有1個兒子。她去世於1412年之後在荷蘭努-布拉班特也-黑達歐根包斯。

zh-hans.'s-Hertogenbosch, North Brabant, Netherl: 荷兰努-布拉班特也-黑達歐根包斯
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth于1370年1月出生在荷兰努-布拉班特利斯时，她的父亲Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel37岁，母亲Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts10岁。她和Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen至少有1个儿子。她去世于1412年之后在荷兰努-布拉班特也-黑達歐根包斯。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Mol
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Dutch Adrianus, Cornelis, Hendrick, Pieter.
Shortened form of Flemish and Dutch   Van Mol  .

Dutch and Flemish: nickname from Middle Dutch mol ‘mole’, perhaps for a very short-sighted or blind person, or someone with dark hair, or a topographic or habitational name referring to a house distinguished by the sign of a mole.

variants.name: Van Mol
meanings: Flemish and Dutch: habitational name for someone from Mol in Antwerp province, Belgium.

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Dorth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably an altered form of German   Dorsch  .

variants.name: Dorsch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Georg, Kurt, Reinhard, Rudi.

North German: metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from Middle High German dorsch ‘cod’ (a borrowing from Middle Low German dorsch, dors(k), itself a loanword from Old Norse thorskr ‘fish for drying’, since cod was traded as dried fish).
South German: from Middle High German torse, the word for a cabbage stalk or rutabaga, hence a nickname for a cabbage or turnip grower or for a tall thin man.

nameMeanings.name: Hadewig
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Manfred.
North German: variant of   Ludwig  .

variants.name: Ludwig
meanings: from hlud ‘fame’ + wīg ‘war’; an ancient royal and imperial name. See Louis .

nameMeanings.name: Jan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

variants.name: John
meanings: Greek: the Lord gave graciously; whom Jehovah gave; whom Jehovah bestowed.

nameMeanings.name: (Ss)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: North German: topographic name for someone living on or owning land that was waterlogged or partly surrounded by water, from Middle Low German es ‘swamp, water’.
Swiss German: from the topographic name Ess ‘grazing area, pasture’.
Swiss German: from a vernacular short form of the personal name   Oswald  .

variants.name: Esse
meanings: male name, from Old French esme ‘esteemed, loved’ (from Latin aestimare ‘to value, esteem’), which was introduced to Scotland in the 16th century. Since the 18th century it has also been in use in the English-speaking world as a female name and it is now much more popular as a name for girls than boys. It occurs in various forms, often written without the accent.

variants.name: Oswald
meanings: From an Old English personal name, derived from ōs ‘god’ + weald ‘rule’. This was the name of two English saints. The first was a 7th-century king of Northumbria, who was killed in battle in 641 . He was a Christian, a convert of St Aidan's , and his opponent, Penda, was a heathen, so his death was counted as a martyrdom by the Christian Church. The second St Oswald was a 10th-century bishop of Worcester and archbishop of York, of Danish parentage, who effected reforms in the English Church. The name was well established in northern England from the late 15th century, particularly in parishes where the church was dedicated to St Oswald. It enjoyed a modest revival in the 19th century as part of the vogue for pre-Conquest English names.

nameMeanings.name: (HELWIG)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from heil ‘whole, safe’ + wīg ‘war’.

en.lifeSummary: When Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth was born in January 1370, in Lith, North Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel, was 37 and her mother, Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts, was 10. She had at least 1 son with Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen. She died after 1412, in 's-Hertogenbosch, North Brabant, Netherlands.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth est née en janvier 1370, à Lith, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts, avait 10 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen. Elle est décédée après 1412, à 's-Hertogenbosch, Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas.

de.lifeSummary: Als Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth im Januar 1370 in Lith, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel 37 und ihre Mutter Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts 10. Sie hatte mit Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb nach 1412 in 's-Hertogenbosch, Nord-Brabant, Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth è nata il nel gennaio 1370 nel Lith, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel, aveva 37 anni e sua madre, Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts, aveva 10 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen. Morì dopo il 1412, nel 's-Hertogenbosch, Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi.

ja.'s-Hertogenbosch, North Brabant, Netherl: オランダノオルド-ブラバント'ス-ハートジェンボッシュ
ja.Lith, North Brabant, Netherlands: オランダノオルド-ブラバントリス
ja.lifeSummary: Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorthが1370年1月にオランダノオルド-ブラバントリスで生まれた時，父Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Drielは37歳，母Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthoutsは10歳であった。彼女はArnoud Floris Jan van der Dussenとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼女は1412年以後にオランダノオルド-ブラバント'ス-ハートジェンボッシュで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth(이)가 1370년 1월 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 리스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel(은)는 37세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts(은)는 10세였다. 그녀는 Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1412년 이후 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트 '스-헤르토겐보슈에서 사망했다.

pt.Lith, North Brabant, Netherlands: Lith, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth nasceu em janeiro de 1370, em Lith, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos, seu pai, Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel, tinha 37 anos e sua mãe, Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts, tinha 10 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen. Ela faleceu após 1412, em 's-Hertogenbosch, Brabante do Norte, Países Baixos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth родилась январь 1370 г. в местности лит, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts, 10. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen. Она умерла после 1412 г. в местности 'с-хиртодженбощ, нурд-брабант, Нидерланды.

es.Lith, North Brabant, Netherlands: Lith, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Hadewig Jan Heimerik (Ss) (HELWIG) van Mol van Dorth nació en enero de 1370, en Lith, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos, su papá, Jan Heimerik Jan van Dordrecht van Driel, tenía 37 años y su mamá, Elisabeth Jan Arnoud Bac van Tilborch Berthouts, tenía 10 años. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Arnoud Floris Jan van der Dussen. Murió después de 1412, en 's-Hertogenbosch, Brabante del Norte, Países Bajos.
